
Scanned arXiv RSS signals and Grok social signals for 2026-04-13. Two arXiv papers stand out as high-confidence additions: 'Artifacts as Memory Beyond the Agent Boundary' addresses state management and memory architecture across agent boundaries, and 'SEA-Eval' provides a benchmark for evaluating self-evolving agents beyond episodic assessment, directly relevant to evaluation loops and iterative refinement. The Grok social signals reference older/existing frameworks (AutoGen 0.4, LangGraph, CrewAI) that are either stale or lack fresh primary technical sources tied to this window. Most other arXiv entries are off-topic (RL optimization, MSO formulas, physical planning).

## Suggestions

- [Artifacts as Memory Beyond the Agent Boundary](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08756) — Examines how artifacts serve as persistent memory structures that extend state management beyond individual agent boundaries, with implications for multi-agent orchestration and long-horizon workflow design.
  Section: Research Papers | Criteria: Architectural guidance for goal-directed agent systems (patterns, orchestration, state management), Tool-use and execution design across agent lifecycle stages (planning, execution, feedback, recovery), Iterative refinement practices (evaluation loops, human-in-the-loop, observability)

- [SEA-Eval: A Benchmark for Evaluating Self-Evolving Agents Beyond Episodic Assessment](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08988) — Proposes a benchmark framework for evaluating self-evolving agents across multi-episode trajectories, providing empirical grounding for evaluation loop design and iterative refinement practices in agentic systems.
  Section: Research Papers | Criteria: Iterative refinement practices (evaluation loops, human-in-the-loop, observability), Empirical or practical grounding (case studies, benchmarks, production deployment evidence), Architectural guidance for goal-directed agent systems (patterns, orchestration, state management)
